methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
OBSIDIA Semiconductor is a seed-stage company in the Chips & Semiconductors sector. The valuation landscape includes a comp universe of 3 public peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers, with notable private companies including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, PsiQuantum, Etched, and Tenstorrent. However, the narrow peer set presents material constraints for traditional valuation triangulation.

The Peer-Median method is not applicable in this analysis. Of 10 same-sector peers identified, none satisfy the filtering criteria for statistical reliability: zero companies match both seed stage and similar valuation tier, one company occupies an adjacent stage, and zero fall within comparable valuation bands. A median derived from the full sector would introduce excessive noise and misrepresent OBSIDIA's positioning within early-stage semiconductor development. Applying across-sector multiples to a capital-intensive, long-cycle hardware business would violate comparability assumptions.

The sparse data environment precludes a defensible triangulated valuation range at this time. To support a rigorous analysis, the dataset would require either (1) additional seed-stage semiconductor companies with disclosed valuations in similar technical verticals, or (2) disclosed fundraising terms from the identified private peers. Without meeting the three-datapoint threshold for statistical relevance, any quantitative synthesis would rest on insufficient evidence. Practitioners should note that early-stage semiconductor valuations are typically driven by technical differentiation, IP position, and founding team pedigree—factors not captured in comparable-company multiples alone.
